bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid for myself. The opponent’s initial offer is valid but only moderately valuable to me, and their threat is cheap talk this early, so I will counter with my strongest preferences across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for me than their opening offer despite their message claiming movement, so I am trusting the offer history over the threat language. I will concede only on the low-weight issueD, where they have repeatedly proposed valueC.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have actually decreased in utility to me rather than improving, so I am not treating their threats as meaningful concessions. I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on the lowest-weight issue to avoid repeating my last exact offer and keep movement visible.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little reason to concede on the three high-weight issues. The opponent’s offers have not improved for me overall and their threatening messages are cheap talk, but they consistently propose issueD:valueC, so I can concede there since issueD has low weight for me.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have not shown meaningful concession in terms of my utility. Their messages are mostly threats and urgency, but the actual bids remain low for me, so I will concede only on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on my most important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility counteroffer around 0.88, conceding mainly on issueD and moderately on issueC while holding firm on the highly important issueA and issueB. Since it is still early at t=0.10 and the opponent’s offers have not improved in utility to me, I should not overreact to their threats or make a large concession yet.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible concession from my ideal on the lower-weight issueD and a moderate concession on issueC. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and their messages are mostly threats, so I am not treating them as binding, but their repeated focus away from my ideal suggests moving slightly without giving up high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 because it is early in the negotiation, but the opponent has not shown meaningful concession toward my highest-value bids. Their threats about deadlock are cheap talk at t=0.14, but their repeated offers suggest they care about issueC and issueD, so I am conceding on those while holding firm on issueA and issueB, which remain high-importance issues for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 because it is early in the negotiation, but I want to show a meaningful concession rather than repeating my last bid. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and are not clearly improving, while their messages are mostly threats, so I am trusting the bid history and conceding on issueD and partially on issueA while holding firm on high-value issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.77 because it is early in the negotiation, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s bids have stayed clustered around low value for me and their threats are cheap talk, but they consistently signal preference away from my ideal on issueA and issueD, so I concede there while holding firm on the high-weight issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility counter around 0.81, since it is still early at t=0.20 and there is no need to drop near the opponent’s current low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s offers have not shown meaningful concession in utility to me, though their latest bid includes issueA=valueB and issueC=valueC, suggesting some movement on issueA while they still resist my top values on issueB and issueC. I will concede issueA and the low-weight issueD, but hold firm on the more valuable issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.77, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier near-optimal bids while still protecting the high-weight issues. At t=0.22 there is still plenty of time, so I should not accept the opponent’s latest 0.616-value offer, but their move to issueB: valueA is a real concession worth reciprocating on issueA and the low-weight issueD. The opponent’s repeated threats are cheap talk; the offer history shows gradual movement, especially on issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, conceding modestly because we are still early-mid negotiation at t=0.24 and there is no need to accept the opponent’s much lower-value current offer. The opponent’s bids are erratic rather than steadily conceding, though they have signaled possible flexibility on issueB and often choose issueD:valueC, so I hold firm on issueB while making a compromise on issueA, issueC, and low-weight issueD. Their repeated threats are cheap talk and are not supported by the actual time pressure yet.

I targeted a utility around 0.66: a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids, but still comfortably above reservation this early-mid stage at t=0.26. The opponent’s actual offers show willingness to give issueB:valueA recently, while they keep pushing weaker outcomes for issueA/issueC and use repeated threats in messages, so I am trusting the bid history over the rhetoric. This counter holds firm on issueB, concedes issueA to valueB, and moves issueC/issueD toward values they have repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.62: below my earlier high-utility demands but still comfortably above the opponent’s latest offer value to me. At t=0.28 there is time left, so I should concede selectively rather than collapse; the opponent’s repeated threats are cheap talk, but their bids show movement toward issueB: valueA while holding pressure on issueA/issueC/issueD, so I concede issueC and issueD while keeping issueA at a middle value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.66: I keep issueB at valueA because the opponent has repeatedly offered it recently and it is highly valuable to me, while conceding on issueA, issueC, and the low-weight issueD. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I do not need to accept the opponent’s lower-utility threats, but their offers show movement toward issueB: valueA and a preference for issueC/valueC-or-D and issueD/valueC-or-D.
